cot_prefix: Okay, so the question is: It's the last day of 1899, and I need to find out what the date will be one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, and I have to pick the correct one. Let me try to work through this step by step. First, the last day of 1899. The year 1899 ends on December 31st. So today is December 31, 1899. Now I need to add one week, which is 7 days, to that date. Let me count the days carefully. Starting from December 31, adding one day would be January 1, 1900. Because the next day after December 31 is the first day of the next year, which is 1900. So adding one day: Jan 1, 1900. Then add another day: Jan 2, 1900. Then Jan 3, 1900. Then Jan 4, 1900. Then Jan 5, 1900. Then Jan 6, 1900. Then Jan 7, 1900. That's a total of 7 days. So one week from December 31, 1899 is January 7, 1900. In MM/DD/YYYY format, that would be 01/07/1900. So the correct option is (D). Let me double-check. Maybe I made a mistake in the counting? Let me recount. December has 31 days. So Dec 31 + 1 day is Jan 1. Then adding 6 more days gets me to Jan 7. Yes, that's 7 days total. So one week later is Jan 7, 1900. So (D) is the correct answer. Let me look at the options again to make sure. Option (D) is 01/07/1900. Yep. So that's the right one.
